data.title: Bitcoin's price drops below $111,000, testing support at $110,700 & setting up CME gap at $117,200 with 80%+ probability.
data.description: Bitcoin experienced a rapid decline of over $4,000 in just a few minutes on Sunday, establishing new support and resistance levels to monitor. Analyst Kevin highlighted in a Patreon post that Bitcoin tested a crucial support area at $110,700, which coincides with the 0.5 Fibonacci retracement level and the daily 100 exponential moving average. This support level removed liquidity pools down to $111,000 and created a significant CME gap upwards at $117,200, historically having an 80%+ chance of being filled. Despite triggering substantial liquidations, the sudden drop on Sunday could be interpreted as more bullish than bearish in the short term, indicating potential for further... For the complete article, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Woodchuck's GM Matthew Wheeler named in Crain's GR Notable Leaders in Sustainability for innovative strategies in clean energy.
data.description: A startup based in Grand Rapids, Michigan called Woodchuck, specializing in converting wood waste into clean energy, celebrates the achievement of their General Manager, Matthew Wheeler. He has been recognized as one of Crain’s Grand Rapids Business Notable Leaders in Sustainability. This acknowledgment highlights his outstanding contributions to sustainability in West Michigan, showcasing innovative approaches and community engagement. The award spotlights individuals who demonstrate leadership in sustainability practices. The selection process involves peer nominations, highlighting the significance of the accomplishment.

data.title: Cryptocurrency exchange-traded products hit $1.4B in losses due to recent sell-offs of Bitcoin, Ether. Investor sentiment polarized.
data.description: According to James Butterfill from CoinShares, Crypto Exchange Traded Products (ETPs) experienced significant losses, the highest since March, with outflows reaching $1.43 billion. This was attributed to a shift in investor sentiment, which has become increasingly divided and "polarized."

data.title: Florida Blue Foundation is accepting grant applications for maternal health initiatives in Florida, aiming to improve outcomes.
data.description: In Jacksonville, Florida, as of August 25, 2025, the Florida Blue Foundation, which is the charitable arm of Florida Blue and a member of the GuideWell family, has officially opened its doors for grant submissions from non-profit organizations dedicated to enhancing maternal health outcomes in the state of Florida.

data.title: Seaman Corporation expands headquarters in Wooster, Ohio, creating 50 new jobs in Wayne County.
data.description: Seaman Corporation has unveiled its plans to begin construction on a new headquarters in Wooster, Ohio, which is set to result in over 50 new job opportunities within Wayne County. This development marks a significant investment in the community by the privately owned manufacturer.

data.title: Mattamy Homes acquires 40 homesites in Wildflower Ranch, Justin, TX, expanding its presence in Dallas-Fort Worth region.
data.description: In Dallas, Texas on August 25, 2025, it was announced that Mattamy Homes, the largest family-owned homebuilder in North America, is increasing its footprint in the Dallas-Fort Worth area by purchasing 40 homesites in Wildflower Ranch. This premier master-planned community is located in Justin, Texas.

data.title: Community Options appoints Derek Shive as Regional Director in Pennsylvania; leading nonprofit supporting disabilities.
data.description: In Harrisburg, Pennsylvania on August 25, 2025, Community Options, a prominent national nonprofit aiding individuals with disabilities, revealed the selection of Derek Shive as the Regional Director for Central Pennsylvania. This strategic move coincides with recent developments within the organization.

data.title: NORTIV 8 marks 9th anniversary by launching Armadillo 3 and expanding across hiking and work gear, celebrating innovation.
data.description: For 9 years and counting, NORTIV 8, the reputable American brand for outdoor and work footwear, continues to push boundaries. On its 9th anniversary, the brand unveils an impressive new collection that sets new standards for comfort and durability. This brand, beloved by millions, remains dedicated to innovation and quality in its products.

data.title: Bohemia Interactive Simulations chosen by Canadian Army for advanced digital training; awarded five-year contract.
data.description: A company known as Bohemia Interactive Simulations (BISim), which is a subsidiary of BAE Systems, Inc., recently secured a significant contract from the Canadian Department of National Defense. This five-year enterprise license agreement is aimed at providing the Digital Virtual Trainer (DVT). The contract indicates a strong commitment for the development of simulation technology in the defense sector, with potential positive outcomes for both parties involved.

data.title: Haloid Solutions to sell ambulances under $150,000, partnering with cost-driven manufacturers for public safety agencies.
data.description: In New York on August 25, 2025, Haloid Solutions announced its commitment to offering competitive prices on emergency vehicles for public safety organizations and government agencies. They are venturing into the ambulance market through collaborations with manufacturers focused on cost efficiency, aiming to deliver value to customers through affordable options.
